what does this error mean in synapse ?
2026-03-26 12:45:26.137 UTC [236339] STATEMENT:  INSERT INTO account_data (user_id, account_data_type, stream_id, content) VALUES ('@s.m.kerkennah_op1:172.19.0.1:8008', 'im.vector.setting.breadcrumbs', 1428985, '{"recent_rooms":["!hmrgUIclVfaJwDMlSX:172.19.0.1:8008","!DbmgMLURNREWJEglrj:172.19.0.1:8008","!EnrLBwGNOXYoiUVpEL:172.19.0.1:8008","!wDUxgDDQtWwIJxxdQb:172.19.0.1:8008","!xgniBNssZZRZjcugym:172.19.0.1:8008","!QqclvimoRLmupDgdpm:172.19.0.1:8008","!UwFQlSjwBaAQEemPHo:172.19.0.1:8008","!mYlBHizkFPAwPDdxLI:172.19.0.1:8008","!fGHHXGgHZYPdGCfBdE:172.19.0.1:8008","!QLnVkXxMPPQFCMRxIZ:172.19.0.1:8008"]}') ON CONFLICT (user_id, account_data_type) DO UPDATE SET stream_id=EXCLUDED.stream_id, content=EXCLUDED.content
2026-03-26 12:55:53.181 UTC [236342] ERROR:  could not serialize access due to concurrent update
2026-03-26 12:55:53.181 UTC [236342] STATEMENT:  DELETE FROM presence_stream WHERE stream_id < 77132328 AND user_id = ANY(ARRAY['@s_ksar_az2:172.19.0.1:8008'])
2026-03-26 12:56:15.564 UTC [236339] ERROR:  could not serialize access due to concurrent update
2026-03-26 12:56:15.564 UTC [236339] STATEMENT:  DELETE FROM presence_stream WHERE stream_id < 77132334 AND user_id = ANY(ARRAY['@s.gabes_op1:172.19.0.1:8008'])
2026-03-26 13:43:43.279 UTC [236428] ERROR:  could not serialize access due to concurrent update
2026-03-26 13:43:43.279 UTC [236428] STATEMENT:  DELETE FROM presence_stream WHERE stream_id < 77145264 AND user_id = ANY(ARRAY['@d.jendouba_op1:172.19.0.1:8008'])
2026-03-26 13:49:11.430 UTC [236420] ERROR:  could not serialize access due to concurrent update
2026-03-26 13:49:11.430 UTC [236420] STATEMENT:  DELETE FROM presence_stream WHERE stream_id < 77146906 AND user_id = ANY(ARRAY['@s.metlaoui_op1:172.19.0.1:8008'])

Synapse PostgreSQL Serialization Error

What's happening

These errors mean PostgreSQL couldn't complete a transaction because another transaction modified the same row(s) at the same time. It's a concurrency conflict — two workers tried to write/delete the same data simultaneously, and Postgres (running in REPEATABLE READ or SERIALIZABLE isolation) aborted one to protect consistency.

You're seeing two distinct patterns:

Error 1 — account_data INSERT (less critical)

INSERT INTO account_data ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE

A user's breadcrumb/recent rooms data was being upserted by two concurrent requests. One lost the race. Synapse typically retries this automatically — usually harmless.

Error 2 — presence_stream DELETE (the main issue)

DELETE FROM presence_stream WHERE stream_id < X AND user_id = ANY(...)

This is the dominant error. Synapse's presence writer worker is trying to purge old presence rows, but multiple workers or processes are hitting the same rows at the same time.

Common causes:

You're running multiple Synapse workers (presence writer + another worker) both trying to manage presence cleanup

A restart/reconnect storm causing many users to flush presence simultaneously

The presence stream table growing large, causing more frequent and overlapping purge attempts

How to fix it

1. Ensure only one presence writer
In your homeserver.yaml, verify only one worker has:

worker_app: synapse.app.generic_worker
# with:
stream_writers:
  presence: [presence_writer]

Only one worker should own presence writes.

2. If not using workers, these are likely just Synapse retrying internally — check if the errors are followed by successful retries (no downstream symptoms = probably fine).

3. Check for multiple Synapse processes

ps aux | grep synapse

Make sure you don't accidentally have duplicate worker processes competing.

4. Tune presence (optional)
If presence is causing load, you can disable it entirely in homeserver.yaml:

use_presence: false

This is common in large deployments where presence tracking isn't needed.

Severity assessment

Error
Severity
Action needed
account_data upsert conflict
🟡 Low
None — Synapse retries
presence_stream delete conflict
🟠 Medium
Check worker config

These won't cause data loss, but repeated serialization failures can cause presence state to be stale and add unnecessary load to your database. Worth addressing if they're frequent.
the problem is currently on my synapse up in the chats all of them i get an error saying connection to the server lost all msg are being stored until connexion comes back

That's a different (but related) symptom. The "connection to server lost" in the Matrix client is a sync/streaming issue, not just a DB conflict. Let's diagnose this properly.

What's likely happening

Your Synapse server is dropping client long-poll connections (/sync requests). Clients show "connection lost" and queue messages locally until they can reconnect.

Most likely causes

1. 🔴 Worker overload / sync handler dying

If you use workers, the sync worker may be crashing or timing out due to the DB serialization errors piling up — a busy presence_stream table causes delays that cascade into /sync timeouts.

2. 🔴 PostgreSQL connection pool exhausted

Too many Synapse workers competing for DB connections. Check:

# How many connections are active right now
psql -U synapse_user -d synapse -c "SELECT count(*), state FROM pg_stat_activity GROUP BY state;"

If you're near your max_connections limit, clients will stall and drop.

3. 🟠 Federation/presence storm consuming resources

The repeated presence_stream conflicts you saw earlier can flood the DB with retries, starving the sync handler of resources.

Immediate checks

Check Synapse logs for the real error:

journalctl -u matrix-synapse --since "1 hour ago" | grep -E "ERROR|WARNING|Exception|504|timeout"

Check if the homeserver is reachable:

curl -s https://YOUR_DOMAIN/_matrix/client/v3/sync?timeout=0 -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"

Check resource usage:

htop  # CPU / RAM
df -h  # Disk — a full disk kills Synapse silently

Check PostgreSQL health:

psql -U synapse -c "SELECT pid, now() - pg_stat_activity.query_start AS duration, query, state FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state != 'idle' ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 10;"

Look for long-running or blocked queries.

Quick mitigations

Restart Synapse (if safe to do so) — clears stuck connections:

systemctl restart matrix-synapse

Disable presence temporarily to stop the serialization storm:

# homeserver.yaml
use_presence: false

Then restart. This alone often resolves both the DB errors and the sync drops.

What to share for deeper diagnosis

If the above doesn't help, paste:

Output of journalctl -u matrix-synapse -n 100 --no-pager

Your worker config (how many workers, which types)

Output of the pg_stat_activity query above

That'll let us pinpoint whether it's DB, network, or a specific worker dying.
i have synapse and synapse-db and element please tell me to run what where to check what is the issue

Got it — simple setup, let's check everything step by step.

Step 1 — Check Synapse logs (run on the Synapse container)

docker logs synapse --tail 100

Look for any ERROR, Exception, or timeout lines. Paste what you see.

Step 2 — Check if Synapse process is healthy

docker ps

Check that all 3 containers show Up and not Restarting or Exited.

Step 3 — Check PostgreSQL connections (run on the synapse-db container)

docker exec -it synapse-db psql -U synapse -d synapse -c "SELECT count(*), state FROM pg_stat_activity GROUP BY state;"

Step 4 — Check for slow/blocked queries

docker exec -it synapse-db psql -U synapse -d synapse -c "SELECT pid, now() - query_start AS duration, state, query FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state != 'idle' ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 10;"

Step 5 — Check disk and memory on the host

df -h
free -h

A full disk or out-of-memory situation will silently kill connections.

Run these one by one and paste the output here — I'll tell you exactly what's wrong and what to fix. 🔍

Good news and bad news. The Synapse logs look healthy — syncs are returning 200, response times are fast (under 0.1sec). The issue is elsewhere.

Now run the DB query. Since you're using Docker:

docker exec -it synapse-db psql -U synapse -d synapse -c "SELECT pid, now() - query_start AS duration, state, query FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state != 'idle' ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 10;"

And also these two at the same time:

# Total connection count
docker exec -it synapse-db psql -U synapse -d synapse -c "SELECT count(*), state FROM pg_stat_activity GROUP BY state;"

# Host resources
df -h && free -h

Also one important question — when exactly do users get "connection lost"?

Run those 3 commands while we wait and paste the results here!

bash-5.1# psql -U synapse -d synapse -c "SELECT pid, now() - query_start AS duration, state, query FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state != 'idle' ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 10;"
  pid   | duration | state  |                                                                 query
--------+----------+--------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 236512 | 00:00:00 | active | SELECT pid, now() - query_start AS duration, state, query FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state != 'idle' ORDER BY duration DESC LIMIT 10;
(1 row)
bash-5.1# psql -U synapse -d synapse -c "SELECT count(*), state FROM pg_stat_activity GROUP BY state;"
 count | state
-------+--------
     5 | 
     1 | active
    10 | idle
(3 rows)

DB is perfectly healthy — only 10 idle connections, no blocked queries, nothing stuck. The database is not your problem.

This means the "connection lost" in Element is almost certainly a network/proxy issue between clients and Synapse.

Run these next:

1. Check if there's a reverse proxy (nginx/traefik) in front of Synapse:

docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Ports}}\t{{.Status}}"

2. Check Synapse's actual network exposure:

docker inspect synapse | grep -A 20 '"Ports"'

3. Check if Element can reach Synapse directly:

curl -s http://localhost:8008/_matrix/client/versions

My strong suspicion right now

Looking at your earlier logs, I noticed this:

All requests come from 10.200.0.2 — that's a single IP, meaning there's a reverse proxy or load balancer in front

Some syncs take exactly 30 seconds (30.139sec, 30.003sec) — that's the sync long-poll timeout being hit, which means the proxy is cutting the connection just at the timeout boundary

This is a classic nginx proxy timeout misconfiguration. The proxy kills the connection before Synapse can respond, and Element sees it as "connection lost."

Do you have nginx, traefik, or caddy running? Paste the output of docker ps and we'll confirm.

yes i have a reverse proxy synapse-ssl infront of synapse the loogs seem healthy :
10.200.0.2 - - [26/Mar/2026:14:21:27 +0000] "GET /_matrix/client/r0/sync?filter=0&set_presence=offline&timeout=6000&since=s1313155_77155116_73120_394419_1429273_1413_390309_61545_1 HTTP/1.1" 200 725 "-" "Agenz/1.4.7-dev (Linux; U; Android 14; SM-X306B Build/UP1A.231005.00; Flavour FDroid; MatrixAndroidSdk2 1.4.2)" "-"

The nginx logs look healthy too — 200s all around. But I need to see the nginx config to confirm the timeout settings, because the logs won't show a timeout problem, only the config will.

Run these:

1. Find the nginx config inside synapse-ssl:

docker exec -it synapse-ssl cat /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

2. Also check the site config:

docker exec -it synapse-ssl ls /etc/nginx/conf.d/
docker exec -it synapse-ssl cat /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf

3. Show me your docker-compose.yml:

cat docker-compose.yml

What I'm looking for specifically

In the nginx config I want to check these values:

Setting
Should be
If missing/low = problem
proxy_read_timeout
120s or higher
Kills long-poll at 60s default
proxy_send_timeout
120s or higher
Drops slow uploads
keepalive_timeout
120s or higher
Drops idle connections
proxy_buffering
off
Buffers break streaming sync

The default nginx proxy_read_timeout is 60 seconds — and your sync requests use a 30s timeout, which should be fine, but if anything adds latency it tips over the edge and clients see "connection lost."

Paste those configs and we'll fix it precisely.
disconnecting qnd connecting qgqin fixed the issue. it  was only on 1 user that had this issue

Ah, that changes everything — if reconnecting fixed it and it was only 1 user, it was almost certainly just a stale/stuck sync connection, not a server problem at all.

What likely happened

That one user's /sync long-poll connection got stuck in a weird state — either:

Their client (browser/app) had a network hiccup that froze the connection without properly closing it

Synapse held onto a dead connection from that client and wouldn't release it

The client got out of sync with the server's stream position and was waiting on a token that already moved on

Reconnecting forced a fresh /sync from a new token — problem gone.

This is normal and not a server issue

A single user needing to reconnect once is expected behavior in Matrix, especially if:

They switched networks (WiFi → mobile)

Their device woke from sleep

A brief internet blip on their end

Your server is actually fine ✅

Component
Status
Synapse
✅ Healthy, fast responses
PostgreSQL
✅ Only 10 connections, no blocked queries
nginx
✅ Returning 200s

The only real thing worth doing is the earlier recommendation — disable presence to stop those serialization errors from accumulating:

# homeserver.yaml
use_presence: false

Then docker restart synapse. That cleans up the DB noise you saw at the start. Everything else is fine.
